task_completion.description: Agent accessed the OpenC3 COSMOS interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open displaying the OpenC3 COSMOS dashboard or login screen.

task_completion.description: Agent opened the Command Sender tool and selected the INST target
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Command Sender' or similar command execution interface is open in COSMOS, with the 'INST' target selected in the dropdown or target list.

task_completion.description: Agent viewed telemetry to verify command responses
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Telemetry Viewer', 'Packet Viewer', or similar telemetry monitoring interface is open, displaying data packets or telemetry points for the INST target.

task_completion.description: Agent executed multiple distinct commands against the INST target
task_completion.visual_evidence: Command history, logs, or active interaction with the 'Send' button showing that multiple (at least 5) commands were sent to the INST target.

task_completion.description: Agent created the required JSON test report file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., VS Code, gedit, nano) or terminal is open showing the creation or editing of the file at '/home/ga/Desktop/test_report.json'.

task_completion.description: The test report follows the required JSON schema and includes at least 5 test cases
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'test_report.json' are visible and show valid JSON with 'test_suite_name': 'INST_CMD_ACCEPTANCE', 'total_tests' >= 5, and an array of 'test_cases' containing 'command', 'expected', 'actual', and 'result' fields.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely interacted with the OpenC3 COSMOS application to send commands and view telemetry.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating and using the COSMOS web interface (or writing a legitimate API script), rather than just typing out a fake JSON report without ever opening COSMOS.

integrity.description: Agent actually checked telemetry rather than hallucinating the 'actual' results in the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be screenshots showing the agent looking at the telemetry viewer after sending commands, correlating with the 'actual' telemetry changes recorded in the JSON report.

integrity.description: Agent used distinct commands as requested, rather than sending the exact same command 5 times.
integrity.visual_evidence: The COSMOS command history or the JSON report should list different commands being executed (e.g., different opcodes, parameters, or subsystems within INST).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target simulator is 'INST'. The agent must send a minimum of 5 commands to the INST target using OpenC3 COSMOS. The final test report must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/Desktop/test_report.json' and must strictly follow the provided JSON schema, including 'test_suite_name': 'INST_CMD_ACCEPTANCE' and an array of at least 5 test cases.
